lede: A look at July 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Francesco Cossiga and Gaël Clichy, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hillary Clinton
events.desc: Hillary Clinton becomes the first female nominee for president of the United States by a major political party at the Democratic National Convention in Philadelphia.

events.title: Nigeria
events.desc: The militant Nigerian Islamist group Boko Haram attacks a police station in Bauchi, leading to reprisals by the Nigeria Police Force and four days of violence across multiple cities.

events.title: Mumbai
events.desc: Mumbai, India receives 99.5cm of rain (39.17 inches) within 24 hours, resulting in floods killing over 5,000 people.

events.title: Asiana Airlines Flight 733
events.desc: Asiana Airlines Flight 733 crashes into a ridge on Mt. Ungeo on its third attempt to land at Mokpo Airport, South Korea. Sixty-eight of the 116 people on board are killed.

events.title: Quebec
events.desc: The National Assembly of Quebec imposes the use of French as the official language of the provincial government.

events.title: Arizona
events.desc: Arizona Governor John Howard Pyle orders an anti-polygamy law enforcement crackdown on residents of Short Creek, Arizona, which becomes known as the Short Creek raid.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of Grand Harbour, British forces on Malta destroy an attack by the Italian Decima Flottiglia MAS. Fort St Elmo Bridge covering the harbour is demolished in the process.

events.title: Buenos Aires
events.desc: In Buenos Aires, Argentina the Revolución del Parque takes place, forcing President Miguel Ángel Juárez Celman's resignation.

events.title: Liberia
events.desc: Liberia declares its independence from the United States. France and the United Kingdom are the first to recognize the new nation.

events.title: French and Indian War
events.desc: French and Indian War: The Siege of Louisbourg ends with British forces defeating the French and taking control of the Gulf of Saint Lawrence.

births.name: Francesco Cossiga
births.line: Italian academic and politician, 8th President of Italy

births.name: Stanley Kubrick
births.line: American director, producer, screenwriter, and cinematographer

births.name: George Bernard Shaw
births.line: Irish playwright and critic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Eva Perón, Argentinian politician, 25th First Lady of Argentina
Archduke Maximilian Francis of Austria, archduke of Austria
Emperor Cheng of Jin, emperor of the Jin Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Andrew of Phú Yên
Christian feast day: Paraskevi of Rome (Eastern Orthodox Church)
